subject: Ancient History
stem: According to Kautilya’s Arthashastra, which of the following are correct? 1. A person could be a slave as a result of a judicial punishment. 2. If a female slave bore her master a son, she was legally free. 3. If a son born to a female slave was fathered by her master the son was entitled to the legal status of the master’s son. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3 only
solution.detail: The Indus Valley Civilization (Harappan Civilisation) flourished between 2600 BC and 1900 BC in the northern region of the Indian subcontinent. It was prominently spread over Sindh, Balochistan, Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Western U.P. and Northern Maharashtra.
solution.trick: For S2, “Only copper and iron” → red flag phrase. Common sense: gold and silver are naturally visible (e.g. riverbeds) — easier to discover than iron. So, saying a developed civilization knew iron but not gold/silver? → logically fishy. Hence likely false. Eliminates option B and D.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: Consider the following pairs: Major Rock Edict Sites: Dhauli (Odisha), Girnar (Gujarat), Mansehra and Shahbazgarhi (Pakistan), Jaugada (Odisha), Erragudi (Andhra Pradesh) Kalsi (Uttarakhand), Sopara (Maharashtra) etc. Minor Rock Edict Sites: Sopara (Maharashtra), Maski, Brahmagiri and Gavimath (Karnataka), Bairat (Rajasthan) etc. Pair 1 is correctly matched : Dhauli is an important site of Ashoka’s Major Rock Edicts in Odisha, located near Bhubaneswar. The inscriptions mention Ashoka’s policy of Dhamma and his remorse after the Kalinga War (261 BCE). The site also features the famous Elephant Carving which symbolizes the emergence of Buddhism. Pair 2 is correctly matched : Erragudi or Yerragudi rock edict is located in Kurnool district in Andhra Pradesh. It mentions Ashoka’s efforts to spread Dhamma (morality and non-violence). The edicts here are inscribed on a granite rock and are written in Brahmi script and Prakrit language. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched : Jaugada is not located in Madhya Pradesh. It is actually located in the Ganjam district of Odisha. It is another significant site of Ashoka’s major rock edicts. The edicts here are written in Brahmi script. Pair 4 is incorrectly matched : Kalsi is actually in Uttarakhand, not Karnataka. It is the only Ashokan Major Rock Edict site in North India. It highlights Ashoka’s message of non-violence, moral conduct, and religious tolerance. The edicts here are inscribed in the Brahmi script and are significant because they mark the northernmost extent of Ashoka’s empire. Site of Ashoka’s major rock edicts Location in the State of A. Dhauli Odisha B. Erragudi Andhra Pradesh C. Jaugada Madhya Pradesh D. Kalsi Karnataka How many pairs given above are correctly matched?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Only one pair
solution.detail: India’s cave architecture is a unique form of ancient rock-cut architecture, primarily serving religious and monastic purposes. Ex- Ajanta Caves, Ellora Caves, Elephanta Caves (Maharashtra), Udayagiri Caves (Madhya Pradesh), Badami Caves (Karnataka) etc. Option (a) is correct: The Ajanta Caves are a UNESCO World Heritage Site located in the Aurangabad district of Maharashtra. They are carved into a horseshoe-shaped cliff along the Waghora River. The caves are renowned for their exquisite Buddhist rockcut architecture and paintings, dating from the 2nd century BCE to the 6th century CE. Option (b) is incorrect: The Sanchi Stupa is located in the Raisen district of Madhya Pradesh near the town of Sanchi. It is not in the gorge of the Chambal River but on a hill near Betwa River. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and contains one of the oldest Buddhist stupas. Option (c) is incorrect: The Pandu-Lena (Trirashmi) Caves are located near Nashik, Maharashtra.The closest river is the Godavari River, not Narmada. These are Buddhist rockcut caves, dating from 1st century BCE to 6th century CE and have inscriptions related to the Satavahanas and Western Kshatrapas. Option (d) is incorrect: The Amaravati Stupa is located in Guntur district, Andhra Pradesh. It is one of the largest Buddhist stupas in India built during the Satavahana period (2nd century BCE). It was rediscovered by Colin Mackenzie in 1797 and located on the banks of the Krishna River, not the Godavari River.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: The Prime Minister recently inaugurated the new Circuit House near Somnath Temple at Veraval. Which of the following statements are correct regarding Somnath Temple? 1. Somnath Temple is one of the Jyotirlinga shrines. 2. A description of Somnath Temple was given by Al-Biruni. 3. Pran Pratishtha of Somnath Temple (installation of the present- day temple) was done by President S. Radhakrishnan. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2 only
solution.detail: The Prime Minister inaugurated and laid the foundation stone of multiple projects in Somnath Temple complex in August 2021. The Somnath temple, also called Deo Patan, is located in Prabhas Patan, Veraval in Gujarat, India. Statement 1 is correct: Somnath Temple, is believed to be the first among the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva. Apart from Somnath, other famous jyotirlingas are Mallikarjuna (Andhra Pradesh), Mahakaleshwar, Omkareshwar (Madhya Pradesh), Bhimashankar (Maharashtra), Baidyanath (Jharkhand), Kashi Vishwanath (Uttar Pradesh) etc. Statement 2 is correct: Al-Biruni, an Arab traveler, gave a highly favorable description of Somanth temple in Kitab Al- Hind (Book of India). It is believed to have inspired Mahmud of Ghazni's expedition in 1024 AD. Statement 3 is incorrect: The contemporary Somnath temple’s reconstruction was started under the orders of the first Home Minister of India, Vallabhbhai Patel. The then President of India, Dr. Rajendra Prasad, did the Pran-Pratistha at the existing temple in 1951.

statements.text: Somnath Temple, is believed to be the first among the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines of Shiva. Apart from Somnath, other famous jyotirlingas are Mallikarjuna (Andhra Pradesh), Mahakaleshwar, Omkareshwar (Madhya Pradesh), Bhimashankar (Maharashtra), Baidyanath (Jharkhand), Kashi Vishwanath (Uttar Pradesh) etc.
Al-Biruni, an Arab traveler, gave a highly favorable description of Somanth temple in Kitab Al- Hind (Book of India). It is believed to have inspired Mahmud of Ghazni's expedition in 1024 AD.

The contemporary Somnath temple’s reconstruction was started under the orders of the first Home Minister of India, Vallabhbhai Patel. The then President of India, Dr. Rajendra Prasad, did the Pran-Pratistha at the existing temple in 1951.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: Which one of the following statements about Sangam literature in ancient South India is correct?
options: Sangam poems are devoid of any reference to material culture.
The social classification of Varna was known to Sangam poets.
Sangam poems have no reference to warrior ethics.
Sangam literature refers to magical forces as irrational.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: The social classification of Varna was known to Sangam poets.
solution.detail: Sangam literature, composed between 300 BCE and 300 CE, is a collection of Tamil poems reflecting ancient South Indian society, culture, and politics. It was composed by poets at the Sangam councils held in Madurai and include contributions from poets like Agastya, Kapilar, and Nakkirar. The literature is divided into Eighteen Greater and Ten Lesser Anthologies, covering themes of love (akam), valor (puram), and ethics. Key texts include Tolkappiyam (grammar), Purananuru (heroic poetry), and Akananuru (love poetry), offering insights into the region’s political, social, and religious life. The two major Tamil epics, Silappathikaram and Manimekalai, are cornerstones of Tamil literature and reflect the cultural and spiritual life of ancient Tamil society Option (b) is correct: Sangam literature does make references to the social classification of Varna, which was a system of social classification in ancient India based on occupation and birth. Sangam Literature provides valuable details about the Sangam society and polity.Social classification of varna was known to Sangam poets.There is mention of social classes like the Arashar (kings), Vaishiyar (traders), and Velalar (farmers).

subject: Art and Culture
stem: With reference to Indian history, consider the following texts: 1. Nettipakarana 2. Parishishta Parvan 3. Avadanasataka 4. Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana Which of the above are Jaina texts?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 4 only
solution.detail:
- Parishishta Parvan also called the Sthaviravali Charita. It was written by Hemachandra (12th century CE). It is a Jain historical text that provides biographies of Jain monks and rulers who supported Jainism. It contains details about Chandragupta Maurya's conversion to Jainism under the guidance of Bhadrabahu.
- Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana is a Jain Purana written by Hemachandra in the 12th century CE. It details the lives of 63 great Jain figures (Shalaka Purushas), including the 24 Tirthankaras, 12 Chakravartins, 9 Vasudevas, 9 Balabhadras, and 9 Prati-Vasudevas.
- Nettipakarana (The Guidebook) is an ancient Pali Buddhist text. It is part of the Khuddaka Nikaya of the Pali Canon and serves as a guide for interpreting Buddhist teachings.
- Avadanasataka(Century of Noble Deeds) is a Buddhist text written in Sanskrit. It is a collection of Buddhist legends and moral stories, emphasizing karmic consequences and rebirth.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: With reference to Indian history, consider the following pairs: Historical person - Known as 1. Aryadeva Jaina scholar 2. Dignaga Buddhist scholar 3. Nathamuni Vaishnavite scholar How many pairs given above are correctly matched?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Only two pairs
solution.detail:
- Āryadeva was a Mahayana Buddhist monk, a disciple of Nagarjuna and a Madhyamaka philosopher. His key work "Catuḥśataka" (Four Hundred Verses) elaborates on Madhyamaka philosophy. His writings are important sources of Madhyamaka in both East Asian Buddhism and Tibetan Buddhism.
- Dignaga, a Buddhist logician and author of the Pramāṇasamuccaya (Compendium of the Means of Valid Knowledge) laid the foundations of Buddhist logic.
- Nathamuni (9th–10th century CE) was a Vaishnavite scholar and theologian. He is credited with compiling the Nalayira Divya Prabandham, a collection of hymns by Tamil Alvars, which became a foundational text in Sri Vaishnavism. He was also the guru of Yamunacharya and predecessor of Ramanuja in the Vishishtadvaita tradition.

solution.detail: On June 21st, the Northern Hemisphere experiences the Summer Solstice. During this time, the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun. The Sun’s rays directly hit the Tropic of Cancer, causing very hot weather. This results in the longest day and the shortest night of the year in the Northern Hemisphere. The North Pole also has continuous daylight for about six months. Meanwhile, in the Southern Hemisphere, it is winter, with longer nights and shorter days.

stem: With reference to foreign-owned e-commerce firms operating in India, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. They can sell their own goods in addition to offering their platforms as market-places. 2. The degree to which they can own big sellers on their platforms is limited. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Under India’s Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) policy, foreign-owned e-commerce firms operating as marketplace models (e.g., Amazon, Flipkart) cannot sell their own goods. They are only allowed to provide a platform for third-party sellers. Statement 2 is correct: The FDI policy limits the degree to which foreign-owned e-commerce firms can own or control sellers on their platforms. For example, a single seller cannot account for more than 25% of the total sales on the platform(as per FDI policy).

statements.text: Under India’s Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) policy, foreign-owned e-commerce firms operating as marketplace models (e.g., Amazon, Flipkart) cannot sell their own goods. They are only allowed to provide a platform for third-party sellers.

The FDI policy limits the degree to which foreign-owned e-commerce firms can own or control sellers on their platforms. For example, a single seller cannot account for more than 25% of the total sales on the platform(as per FDI policy).

solution.trick: If foreign e-commerce players (like Amazon, Walmart-Flipkart) were allowed to sell their own goods directly, they would become inventory-based, not marketplaces. This would hurt Indian MSMEs and create conflict of interest. Also, if they can't even hold large stakes in sellers (S2), then how can they sell their own goods directly(S1)? Both cannot be true at once. If S2 is true, S1 must be false. stem: With reference to the Indian economy, consider the following statements: 1. If the inflation is too high, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is likely to buy government securities. 2. If the rupee is rapidly depreciating, RBI is likely to sell dollars in the market. 3. If interest rates in the USA or European Union were to fall, that is likely to induce RBI to buy dollars. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: When inflation is higher than the RBI aims to reduce liquidity in the market. Buying government securities injects money into the economy by increasing liquidity, which would worsen inflation. On the contrary the RBI is more likely to sell government securities to absorb excess liquidity. Statement 2 is correct: When the rupee depreciates rapidly its value falls relative to the U.S. dollar. This means more rupees are required to purchase one dollar, indicating a weakening rupee. To stabilize the rupee and prevent excessive depreciation, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) can intervene in the foreign exchange market by selling dollars from its reserves. When the RBI sells dollars in the market, it increases the available supply of dollars in the foreign exchange market. Market participants (importers, banks, and investors) exchange rupees to buy these newly available dollars. This increases the demand for rupees. As demand for rupees rises, the pressure on the rupee to depreciate reduces. This can help stabilize or even strengthen the rupee. Statement 3 is correct: A fall in US or EU interest rates makes Indian assets more attractive, prompting foreign investors to seek higher returns in India. stem: Consider the following statements: 1. In India, credit rating agencies are regulated by the Reserve Bank of India. 2. The rating agency popularly known as ICRA is a public limited company. 3. Brickwork Ratings is an Indian credit rating agency. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3 only
solution.detail: Credit Rating is assessment of the creditworthiness of a borrower. Individuals are given ‘credit scores’, while corporations and governments receive ‘credit ratings’. Statement 1 is incorrect: Credit rating agencies in India are regulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) under SEBI (Credit Rating Agencies) Regulations, 1999, not the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). Statement 2 is correct: ICRA (Investment Information and Credit Rating Agency of India Ltd.) was set up in 1991 by IFCI, LIC, SBI and select banks as well as financial institutions to rate debt instruments. The ICRA consists of a group of Companies including its subsidiaries. ICRA Limited is a public limited company listed on stock exchanges. Statement 3 is correct: In India credit rating agencies registered under Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) are CRISIL, ICRA, CARE, SMERA, Fitch India and Brickwork Ratings. Globally, Fitch Ratings, Moody’s Investors Service and Standard & Poor’s (S&P) control approximately 95% of ratings business.

solution.detail: The Banks Board Bureau (BBB) was formed on the recommendations of ‘P. J. Nayak Committee to Review Governance of Boards of Banks. Statement 3 is correct: The BBB advises PSBs on vario-us strategic matters such as: Business strategies,Capital raising plans,Governance reforms,etc. It also assists banks in addressing issues related to non-performing assets (NPAs) and improving operational efficiency.

subject: Economy
stem: In India, which one of the following is responsible for maintaining price stability by controlling inflation?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Reserve Bank of India
solution.detail: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is primarily responsible for maintaining price stability and controlling inflation in India. It does this through its monetary policy framework, which includes tools like the repo rate, reverse repo rate, open market operations, and cash reserve ratio (CRR). Under the Monetary Policy Framework Agreement (2016), the RBI, in collaboration with the Government of India has a mandate to maintain inflation at 4% (with a tolerance band of ±2%), i.e., between 2% to 6%.

subject: Economy
stem: Which one of the following situations best reflects “Indirect Transfers” often talked about in media recently with reference to India?
options: An Indian company investing in a foreign enter-prise and paying taxes to the foreign country on the profits arising out of its investment.
A foreign company investing in India and paying taxes to the country of its base on the profits arising out of its investment.
An Indian company purchases tangible assets in a foreign country and sells such assets after their value increases and transfers the proceeds to India.
A foreign company transfers shares and such shares derive their substantial value from assets located in India.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: A foreign company transfers shares and such shares derive their substantial value from assets located in India.
solution.detail: The term “Indirect Transfers” in the Indian context refers to situations where a foreign entity transfers shares, and those shares derive significant value from assets located in India. This concept gained prominence due to high-profile tax cases such as Vodafone and Cairn Energy. Case Study: In 2007, Vodafone International Holdings BV acquired shares of a Cayman Islands-based company which indirectly owned a majority stake in Indian telecom operator Hutchison Essar Limited. Although this transaction took place outside India between two foreign entities, India’s tax authorities argued that it involved the indirect transfer of Indian assets and demanded capital gains tax. Vodafone challenged this tax demand and the Supreme Court of India (2012) ruled in Vodafone’s favor, stating that indirect transfers were not taxable under Indian law at the time. The Indian government later amended tax laws retroactively, reinforcing its authority to tax such transactions. This led to international arbitration, which again ruled in Vodafone’s favor, citing unfair taxation practices. 213. The money multiplier in an economy increases with which one of the following? subject: Economy
stem: With reference to the expenditure made by an organization or a company, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. 2. Acquiring new technology is capital expenditures. Debt financing is considered capital expenditure, while equity financing is considered revenue expenditure. Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: When a company uses its funds to acquire or upgrade physical assets, it is called Capital Expenditure (CapEx). These assets can include property, plants, buildings, technology, or equipment. CapEx is intended to provide long-term benefits to the organization. Statement 2 is incorrect: When a company borrows money to be paid back at a future date with interest it is known as debt financing. It is not a capital expenditure. Equity financing is the process of raising capital through the sale of shares. It is an example of non-debt capital receipts, not revenue expenditure.

statements.text: When a company uses its funds to acquire or upgrade physical assets, it is called Capital Expenditure (CapEx). These assets can include property, plants, buildings, technology, or equipment. CapEx is intended to provide long-term benefits to the organization.

When a company borrows money to be paid back at a future date with interest it is known as debt financing. It is not a capital expenditure. Equity financing is the process of raising capital through the sale of shares. It is an example of non-debt capital receipts, not revenue expenditure.

solution.extraEdge:
Capital Expenditure (CapEx): refers to the funds a company spends on acquiring, upgrading, or maintaining physical assets such as property, buildings, technology, machinery, or equipment. These expenditures are intended to provide long-term benefits to the company by improving its production capacity, efficiency, or overall infrastructure.
- Equity Financing: Equity financing is the process of raising capital through the sale of shares. It is an example of non-debt capital receipts.
- Debt financing refers to the process of raising capital for a company by borrowing money. In this type of financing, the company takes on a loan or issues bonds to investors, promising to repay the borrowed amount along with interest over a specified period.

subject: Economy
stem: With reference to Indian economy, consider the following statements: 1. A share of the household financial savings goes towards government borrowings. 2. Dated securities issued at market related rates in auctions form a large component of internal debt. Indian Economy 299 Which of the above statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: In India, people save through various financial options, including bank deposits, pension funds, insurance schemes, and government securities. Some of these savings are invested in government bonds and securities, helping the government fund its expenditures and manage its budget shortfall. Thus a portion of household financial savings is used to support government borrowing. Statement 2 is correct: The Indian government raises money by issuing dated securities, which are long-term bonds with a fixed maturity date. Their interest rates are decided by market demand and supply. The government sells these securities through auctions, where banks, financial institutions, and investors place bids. These bonds make up a large part of the government’s internal debt, which is the money it owes to lenders within the country. The share of marketable securities in internal debt is at 76.4 per cent at end-March 2023 which is slightly moderate relative to 76.9 per cent at end-March 2022. subject: Economy
stem: “Rapid Financing Instrument” and “Rapid Credit Facility” are related to the provisions of lending by which one of the following?
options: United Nations Environment Programme Finance Initiative

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: International Monetary Fund
solution.detail:
Rapid Financing Instrument (RFI) and Rapid Credit Facility (RCF) are instruments of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) which provides financial assistance to the countries in need.
- Rapid Financing Instrument (RFI): The Rapid Financing Instrument (RFI) provides rapid financial assistance, which is available to any IMF member countries facing an urgent balance of payment needs. The RFI was created as part of a broader reform to make the IMF’s financial support more flexible to address the diverse needs of member countries. It is one of the facilities under the General Resources Account (GRA) that provide financial support to countries, including in times of crisis.
- Rapid Credit Facility (RCF): The Rapid Credit Facility (RCF) provides rapid concessional financial assistance to low-income countries (LICs) facing an urgent Balance of Payments (BoP) need with no ex post conditionality where a full-fledged economic program is neither necessary nor feasible. The RCF was created under the Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust (PRGT) as part of a broader reform to make the Fund’s financial support more flexible and better tailored to the diverse needs of LICs, including in times of crisis.

subject: Economy
stem: With reference to the Indian economy, consider the following statements: 1. An increase in Nominal Effective Exchange Rate (NEER) indicates the appreciation of rupee. 2. An increase in the Real Effective Exchange Rate (REER) indicates an improvement in trade competitiveness. 3. An increasing trend in domestic inflation relative to inflation in other countries is likely to cause an increasing divergence between NEER AND REER. Which of the statements are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The Nominal Effective Exchange Rate (NEER) is an unadjusted weighted average rate at which a country’s currency exchanges for a basket of multiple foreign currencies. An increase in NEER signifies that the domestic currency has strengthened or appreciated relative to the selected basket of foreign currencies. An increase in NEER means that, on average, the rupee has become more valuable relative to those other currencies. It takes more of the foreign currencies to buy one unit of the rupee. Statement 2 is incorrect: Real Effective Exchange Rate (REER) is a measure of a country’s currency value relative to a basket of other currencies adjusted for inflation differentials. An increase in REER implies that the domestic currency has appreciated in real terms, making exports more expensive and imports cheaper. Consequently, a higher REER indicates a loss in trade competitiveness, not an improvement. Statement 3 is correct: If domestic inflation is higher than inflation in other countries, the REER will appreciate (become more expensive) even if the NEER remains constant or depreciates. This divergence occurs because the REER calculation accounts for inflation differentials. The higher domestic inflation makes a country’s goods less competitive, even if nominal exchange rates don’t reflect that.

stem: With reference to the Indian economy, what are the advantages of “Inflation-Indexed Bonds (IIBs)”? 1. The government can reduce the coupon rates on its borrowing by way of IIBs. 2. IIBs provide protection to the investors from uncertainty regarding inflation. 3. The interest received as well as capital gains on IIBs are not taxable. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2 only
solution.detail: Statements 1 and 2 are correct: Inflation-Indexed Bonds is a debt market securities offered by the government to protect the savings from inflation and offer positive real rates of returns. Since Inflation-Indexed Bonds (IIBs) provide inflation protection to investors, the government can offer these bonds with lower coupon rates compared to traditional bonds. The inflation adjustment compensates for the lower fixed interest. Statement 3 is incorrect: The existing tax provisions will be applicable on interest payment and capital gains on IIBs. There will be no special tax treatment for these bonds.

subject: Economy
stem: Which of the following activities constitute a real sector in the economy? 1. Farmers harvesting their crops 2. Textile mills converting raw cotton into fabrics 3. A commercial bank lending money to a trading company 4. A corporate body issuing Rupee Denominated Bonds overseas. Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2 only
solution.detail:
The real sector refers to the segment of the economy that is involved in the production and consumption of tangible goods and services. It includes industries such as agriculture, manufacturing, construction, mining, and services like healthcare, education, and transportation. Statements 1 and 2 are correct:
- Farmers harvesting their crops: This is a primary sector activity directly contributing to the production of goods (agricultural produce).
- Textile mills converting raw cotton into fabrics: This is a manufacturing activity, part of the secondary sector, which also belongs to the real sector. Statements 3 and 4 are incorrect:
- A commercial bank lending money to a trading company: This is a financial sector activity, involving monetary transactions rather than the production of tangible goods or services.
- A corporate body issuing Rupee Denominated Bonds overseas: This relates to the financial sector and capital markets, as it involves raising funds rather than producing goods or services.

subject: Economy
stem: With reference to Convertible Bonds, consider the following statements: 1. As there is an option to exchange the bond for equity, Convertible Bonds pay a lower rate of interest. 2. The option to convert to equity affords the bondholder a degree of indexation to rising consumer prices. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: A convertible bond is a fixed-income corporate debt security that yields interest payments, but can be converted into a predetermined number of common stock or equity shares. Convertible bonds generally offer a lower coupon rate or rate of return in exchange for the value of the option to convert the bond into common stock. Investors will generally accept a lower coupon rate on a convertible bond, compared with the coupon rate on an otherwise identical regular bond, because of its conversion feature. Statement 2 is correct: Equity (stock) prices tend to rise with inflation. Companies’ earnings and assets often appreciate during inflationary periods. Therefore, the option to convert a bond into equity provides a hedge against inflation. If inflation rises and the company performs well, its stock price is likely to increase. The bondholder can then convert the bond into equity at a more favorable price, thus benefiting from the rising prices and protecting their investment’s real value (purchasing power). This acts as a form of indexation (linking to a price index) against inflation.

stem: Which of the following are nitrogen-fixing plants? 1. Alfalfa 2. Amaranth 3. Chickpea 4. Clover 5. Purslane (Kulfa) 6. Spinach Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1, 3 and 4 only
solution.detail: Alfalfa, chickpea, and clover are nitrogen-fixing plants. They belong to the leguminous family and form symbiotic associations with Rhizobium bacteria in their root nodules, aiding in nitrogen fixation and enriching soil fertility. Amaranth, purslane (Kulfa), and spinach, are non-leguminous plants and lack the ability to fix nitrogen as they do not form symbiotic relationships with nitrogen-fixing bacteria or develop root nodules. This natural ability of legumes like alfalfa, chickpea, and clover to fix nitrogen is extensively utilized in crop rotation practices to replenish soil nutrients and enhance agricultural productivity.

subject: Environment
stem: Among the following crops, which one is the most important anthropogenic source of both methane and nitrous oxide?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Rice
solution.detail: Methane (CH4) and nitrous oxide (N2O) are the two most important GHGs after carbon dioxide (CO2). The most rapid increase in CH4 emission was found in natural wetlands and rice fields due to increased rice cultivation area and climate warming. N2O emission increased substantially in all the biome types and the largest increase occurred in upland crops due to increasing air temperature and nitrogen fertilizer use. Rice paddies are considered one of the most important sources of CH4 and N2O emissions, which have attracted considerable attention due to their contribution to global warming.

subject: Environment
stem: “System of rice Intensification” of cultivation, in which alternate wetting and drying of rice fields is practised, results in: 1. Reduced seed requirement 2. Reduced methane production 3. Reduced electricity consumption Select the correct answer using the code given below

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail:
The System of Rice Intensification involves cultivating rice with as much organic manure as possible, starting with young seedlings planted singly at wider spacing in a square pattern; and with intermittent irrigation that keeps the soil moist but not inundated, and frequent inter cultivation with weeder that actively aerates the soil. SRI is not a standardised, fixed technological method. It is rather a set of ideas, a methodology for comprehensively managing and conserving resources by changing the way that land, seeds, water, nutrients, and human labour are used to increase productivity from a small but well-tended number of seeds. Advantages of SRI Disadvantages of SRI

- Higher labour costs in the initial years

- Difficulties in acquiring the necessary skills
- Reduced seed requirement
- Not suitable when no irrigation source available
- Lesser chemical inputs
- Less water requirement
- Reduced electricity consumption

- rain weight increased without change in grain size

subject: Environment
stem: In the context of WHO Air Quality Guidelines, consider the following statements: 1. The 24-hour mean of PM2.5 should not exceed 15 ug/m3 and annual mean of PM2.5 should not exceed 5 ug/m3. 2. In a year, the highest levels of ozone pollution occur during the period of inclement weather. 3. PM10 can penetrate the lung barrier and enter the bloodstream. 4. Excessive ozone in the air can trigger asthma. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1 and 4 only
solution.detail: The updated (2021) WHO Global Air Quality Guidelines (AQGs) provide recommendations on air quality guideline levels as well as interim targets for six key air pollutants. The guidelines focus on so-called classical pollutants, particulate matter (PM₂.₅ and PM₁₀), ozone (O₃), nitrogen dioxide (NO₂), sulfur dioxide (SO₂) and carbon monoxide (CO). Statement 1 is correct: The 24-hour ceiling mean of PM 2.5 used to be 25 micrograms but has now dropped to 15. Statement 2 is incorrect: Ozone at ground level is formed by the reaction with sunlight (photochemical reaction) of pollutants such as nitrogen oxides (NOx) from vehicle and industry emissions and volatile organic compounds (VOCs) emitted by vehicles, solvents and industry. As a result, the highest levels of ozone pollution occur during periods of sunny weather and not during inclement weather. Statement 3 is incorrect: Particles with a diameter of 10 μm (PM10) can reach the lungs but cannot enter the bloodstream. However, particles with a diameter of 2.5 μm or less can penetrate deeper into the lungs, reaching the alveoli, enter the bloodstream, and potentially cross blood-organ barriers, impacting various organs such as the brain, heart, liver, and kidneys. PM₁₀ (≤10 microns) can reach deep into the lungs, but the more harmful PM₂.₅ (≤2.5 microns) can penetrate the lung barrier and enter the bloodstream, posing severe health risks. Statement 4 is correct: Excessive ozone in the air can have a marked effect on human health. It causes breathing problems, triggers asthma, reduces lung function and causes lung diseases. Ozone triggers asthma because it is very irritating to the lungs and airways.

subject: Environment
stem: Consider the following: 1. Carbon monoxide 2. Nitrogen oxide 3. Ozone 4. Sulphur dioxide Excess of which of the above in the environment is/are the cause(s) of acid rain?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 4 only
solution.detail: Normal rain has a pH of about 5.6 when pH falls below this; it is called Acid rain (pH 4.2 to 4.4). Acid rain results when Sulfur Dioxide (SO2) & Nitrogen Oxides (NOX) react with water, oxygen & other chemicals to form Sulfuric & Nitric acids. Nitrous oxide and Sulphur dioxide cause Acid rain. The acid rain harms microorganisms in the soil; inhibits the activity of nitrogen fixation bacteria; soil acidification; ocean acidification; affects the growth of plants; food chain; kills aquatic animals; corrosion of metals & weathering of stone buildings & statues. NOx + SO2 + Moisture + other chemicals → Acid Rain (HNO3 + H2SO4)

The Under2 Coalition is a global alliance of state and regional governments committed to reducing greenhouse gas emissions. Its Secretariat is managed by Climate Group.

stem: "Climate Action Tracker" which monitors the emission reduction pledges of different countries is a:
options: Database created by coalition of research organisations
Wing of "International Panel of Climate Change"
Committee under "United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change"
Agency promoted and financed by United Nations Environment Programme and World Bank

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Database created by coalition of research organisations
solution.detail:
Option (a) is correct: The Climate Action Tracker (CAT) is an independent scientific project that monitors government climate action and measures it against the globally agreed Paris Agreement aim of "holding warming well below 2°C, and pursuing efforts to limit warming to 1.5°C." It is a collaboration between Climate Analytics and the New Climate Institute. CAT has been providing assessments since 2009. It evaluates countries' emission reduction pledges, policies, and their impact on global warming. Options (b), (c) and (d) are incorrect:
- The Climate Action Tracker is neither a part of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) nor the UNFCCC. The IPCC is a separate body that assesses scientific, technical, and socio-economic information related to climate change.
- The UNFCCC is an international treaty that provides the framework for global climate negotiations.
- The Climate Action Tracker is not funded or promoted by the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) or the World Bank. It is an independent initiative run by research organizations.

stem: The “Miyawaki method” is well known for the:
options: Promotion of commercial farming in arid and semi-arid areas
Development of gardens using genetically modified flora
Creation of mini forests in urban areas
Harvesting wind energy on coastal areas and on sea surfaces

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Creation of mini forests in urban areas
solution.detail:
Miyawaki method is a method of urban afforestation by turning backyards into mini-forests. It includes planting trees as close as possible in the same area which not only saves space, but the planted saplings also support each other in growth and block sunlight from reaching the ground, thereby preventing the growth of weeds. Thus the saplings become maintenance-free (self-sustainable) after the first three years. It helps to create a forest in just 20 to 30 years while through conventional methods it takes anywhere between 200 to 300 years. In the Miyawaki Process:
- The native trees of the region are identified and divided into four layers — shrub, sub-tree, tree, and canopy.
- The quality of soil is analysed and biomass which would help enhance the perforation capacity, water retention capacity, and nutrients in it, is mixed with it. The ground is covered with a thick layer of mulch.
- A mound is built with the soil and the seeds are planted at a very high density — three to five sapling per square meter.

stem: “If rainforests and tropical forests are lungs of the Earth, then surely wetlands function as its kidneys.” Which one of the following functions of wetlands best reflects the above statements?
options: The water cycle in wetlands involves surface runoff, subsoil percolation and evaporation.
Algae form the nutrient base upon which fish, crustaceans, molluscs, birds, reptiles and mammals thrive.
Wetlands play a vital role in maintaining sedimentation balance and soil stabilisation.
Aquatic plants absorb heavy metals and excess nutrients.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Aquatic plants absorb heavy metals and excess nutrients.
solution.detail: Natural wetlands are often called "earth's kidneys" because they effectively filter pollutants from water, much like our kidneys filter waste and excess fluid from our bodies. Just as kidneys maintain a healthy balance of water, salts, and minerals, wetlands absorb and store excess nutrients, heavy metals, and chemicals from runoff. Aquatic plants and microorganisms help absorb heavy metals, excess nutrients, while sediment settles at the bottom in areas of slow-moving water, preventing it from reaching open water.

stem: With reference to “Gucchi” sometimes mentioned in the news, consider the following statements: 1. It is a fungus. 2. 3. It grows in some Himalayan forest areas. It is commercially cultivated in the Himalayan foothills of north-eastern India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Guchhi mushroom is a species of fungus in the family Morchellaceae of the Ascomycota. They are prized for their unique, honeycomb-like appearance. stem: In the northern hemisphere, the longest day of the year normally occurs in the:
options: First half of the month of June
Second half of the month of June
First half of the month of July
Second half of the month of July

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: First half of the month of July
solution.detail: Assertion (A) is true: The Moon is tidally locked to the Earth, meaning that the same hemisphere of the Moon is always facing Earth. This occurs because the Moon’s rotational period (the time it takes to spin once on its axis) is equal to its orbital period (the time it takes to complete one revolution around the Earth). As a result, only about 59% of the Moon’s surface is ever visible from Earth due to lunar libration (a slight wobbling motion). The remaining 41% is permanently hidden from direct view from Earth and is called the far side of the Moon (often incorrectly referred to as the “dark side”). Reason (R) is false: The actual time the Moon takes to rotate on its own axis is 27.3 days, not 23.5 days. The Moon’s orbital period around the Earth is also 27.3 days (called the sidereal month) not 23 and a half days. Since these two periods are equal, the same face of the Moon always faces Earth, which is a result of synchronous rotation (tidal locking)

At the same time, they trap some of the outgoing infrared radiation emitted by the Earth and radiate it back downward, thereby warming the surface of the Earth.

stem: With reference to India, consider the following statements: 1. Monazite is a source of rare earths. 2. Monazite contains thorium. 3. solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1, 2 and 4 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 and 2 are correct: In India, monazite serves as the primary source of rare earth elements and thorium. Rare earth metals comprise a group of 17 elements known for their lustrous, silvery-white appearance and soft, heavy metallic properties. China dominates global rare earth production, contributing around 60-70 % of the total output. These elements play a critical role in the production of electronics, magnets, and batteries. Monazite, abundant in India’s coastal sands, is rich in rare earth elements and thorium, a radioactive material. Thorium is crucial for India’s three stage nuclear energy program. Statement 3 is incorrect: Monazite is not found in the entire Indian coastal sands. While it is found along most of the southern Indian coast, particularly in Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Orissa, it does not occur throughout the entire coastline. Statement 4 is correct: As per Atomic Energy (Radiation Protection) Rules 2004, Indian Rare Earths Limited (IREL), a wholly owned Public Sector Undertaking of the Government of India (GOI) under Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), is the only entity which has been permitted to produce and process monazite, and handle it for domestic use as well as for export. stem: Gandikota canyon of South India was created by which one of the following rivers?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Pennar
solution.detail: Option (c) is correct: Gandikota canyon is situated on the Pennar River in Andhra Pradesh and is known as the Grand Canyon of India. Gandikota is a small village in the Kadapa district of Andhra Pradesh. Gorges are formed when rivers erode through gigantic rock formations over a long period of time. A gorge is any valley formed by a source of water flowing between tall slabs of steep rocks. The swift flowing waters of River Pennar cut through the granite rocks near the village of Gandikota, naturally eroding the rock and forming a deep valley.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one of the following lakes of West Africa has become dry and turned into a desert ?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Lake Faguibine
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: Lake Victoria, located in East Africa (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania), is the largest lake in Africa. It remains full and functional, supporting millions of people and diverse ecosystems. Option (b) is correct: Lake Faguibine in Mali, West Africa, has mostly dried up due to climate change, low rainfall, and human activities. It was once one of the largest lakes in West Africa, covering 590 km², but has shrunk since the 1970s due to severe droughts. The lake depends on seasonal flooding from the Niger River, but with less water, the area has turned into dry land. This has badly affected farming, fishing, and the livelihoods of people who relied on the lake. Option (c) is incorrect: Lake Oguta, located in Nigeria, is a permanent freshwater lake. It has not dried up and continues to serve as an important water source for local communities. Option (d) is incorrect: Lake Volta, located in Ghana, is one of the largest artificial reservoirs in the world. It was created by the construction of the Akosombo Dam on the Volta River. The lake remains full and continues to be a vital source of hydroelectric power, irrigation, and fishing.

subject: International Relations
stem: With reference to the United Nations Convention on the Law of Sea, consider the following statements: 1. A coastal state has the right to establish the breadth of its ter- ritorial sea up to a limit not exceeding 12 nautical miles, mea- sured from baseline determined in accordance with the con- vention. 2. Ships of all states, whether coastal or land-locked, enjoy the right of innocent passage through the territorial sea. 3. The Exclusive Economic Zone shall not extend beyond 200 nautical miles from the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: The United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), 1982, defines maritime zones and rights of coastal and landlocked states. Statement 1 is correct: According to Article 3 of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), every coastal state has the right to establish its territorial sea up to a maximum breadth of 12 nautical miles from baselines determined under the convention. The state exercises full sovereignty over this zone, including its airspace and seabed. Statement 2 is correct: Article 17 of UNCLOS provides that all states, including landlocked ones, have the right of “innocent passage” through the territorial sea of a coastal state. Innocent passage means passing through the sea without harming the security, peace, or environment of the coastal state. Statement 3 is correct: Article 57 of UNCLOS specifies that an Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) extends up to 200 nautical miles from the baseline used to measure the territorial sea. In this zone, the coastal state has special rights over marine resources (fishing, oil exploration, etc.) but it cannot prohibit navigation or overflight by foreign states.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following countries: 1. Armenia 2. Azerbaijan 3. Croatia 4. Romania 5. Uzbekistan Which of the above are members of the Organization of Turkic States?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2 and 5
solution.detail:
Organization of Turkic States (OTS) formerly known as the Turkic Council was established in 2009 under the Nakhchivan Agreement. Its primary objective is to promote comprehensive cooperation among Turkic-speaking countries, fostering collaboration based on shared history, culture, and language. Full Members include Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Türkiye and Uzbekistan and Observer States are Hungary, Turkmenistan, and the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus (TRNC). The OTS Secretariat is headquartered in Istanbul, Türkiye. Options (c) is correct:
- Azerbaijan is a full member of OTS. It has strong ethnic, linguistic, and cultural ties with Turkey and other Turkic nations.
- Uzbekistan is a full member of OTS, joining officially in 2019. It is a Turkic-speaking Central Asian country with historical connections to the Turkic world. Armenia is not a Turkic country and is not a member of OTS. It has historical conflicts with Azerbaijan, especially over Nagorno-Karabakh. Croatia is not a Turkic-speaking country and not a member of OTS. It is part of the European Union (EU) and NATO. Romania is not a Turkic-speaking country and not a member of OTS. It has cultural ties with Hungary and the Balkans but not with the Turkic world.

stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Bidibidi is a large refugee settlement in north-western Kenya. 2. Some people who fled from South Sudan civil war live in Bidibi- di. 3. solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2 and 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Bidibidi is not in Kenya. It is one of the largest refugee settlements in the world located in north-western Uganda, near the border with South Sudan. Statement 2 is correct: Bidibidi was established in 2016 to accommodate refugees fleeing the civil war in South Sudan. Bidibidi refugee settlement in Uganda shelters people displaced by the South Sudan civil war (2013–2018). Uganda hosts over 1 million South Sudanese refugees with Bidibidi being the largest settlement. Statement 3 is correct: The Dadaab refugee complex, located in north-eastern Kenya, has been a major shelter for Somali refugees since the outbreak of civil war in Somalia in 1991. It remains one of the largest and longest-standing refugee camps globally.

subject: International Relations
stem: With reference to the United Nations General Assembly, consider the following statements : 1. The UN General Assembly can grant observer status to the non-member States. 2. Inter-governmental organisations can seek observer status in the UN General Assembly. 3. Permanent Observers in the UN General Assembly can main- tain missions at the UN headquarters. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: The United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) is one of the six principal organs of the United Nations (UN). It is responsible for granting observer status to non-member entities including states and intergovernmental organizations (IGOs). Statement 1 is correct: The UNGA can grant observer status to non-member states. Having observer status allows them to participate in its sessions and work without voting rights. For example, the Holy See (Vatican City) and Palestine have Permanent Observer status in the UN. Statement 2 is correct: Inter-governmental organizations (IGOs) can apply for observer status in the UNGA. Examples: European Union (EU), African Union (AU) and International Organization for Migration (IOM) have observer status at the UN. Statement 3 is correct: Permanent Observers at the UNGA can establish diplomatic missions at the UN Headquarters in New York. Example: The Holy See and Palestine maintain Permanent Observer Missions at the UN.

stem: With reference to the “United Nations Credentials Committee”, consider the following statements: 1. It is a committee set up by the UN Security Council and works under its supervision. 2. It traditionally meets in March, June and September every year. 3. It assesses the credentials of all UN members before submit- ting a report to the General Assembly for approval. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 3 only
solution.detail: The United Nations Credentials Committee is a subsidiary committee of the UN General Assembly (UNGA) and not the UN Security Council. It is responsible for reviewing the credentials of representatives of UN member states and reporting its findings to the General Assembly. Statement 1 is incorrect: The Credentials Committee is established by the UN General Assembly (UNGA), not the UN Security Council (UNSC). It operates independently of the UNSC and reports directly to the General Assembly. Statement 2 is incorrect: There is no fixed schedule for the Credentials Committee. It usually meets before the annual General Assembly session in September and whenever necessary. The statement that it meets three times a year (March, June, and September) is not correct. Statement 3 is correct: The committee reviews and validates the credentials of representatives of all UN member states. It then submits a report to the General Assembly which then votes on whether to accept or reject the credentials. This process ensures that only legitimate representatives of member states participate in UN meetings and decisions.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following: 1. Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank 2. Missile Technology Control Regime 3. Shanghai Cooperation Organisation India is a member of which of the above?

solution.detail: Option 1 is correct: Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) is a multilateral development bank headquartered in Beijing, China. It focuses on infrastructure financing in the Asia-Pacific region. India is a founding member (member since 2016) and the second-largest shareholder after China. Option 2 is correct: Missile Technology Control Regime (MTCR) is an informal political agreement among 35 member countries to prevent the proliferation of missile technology capable of carrying WMDs (weapons of mass destruction). India became a full member in 2016, helping strengthen its missile development programs. Option 3 is correct: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) is a Eurasian political, economic, and security alliance originally led by China and Russia. India joined as a full member in 2017 alongside Pakistan.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Vietnam has been one of the fastest-growing economies in the world in the recent years. 2. Vietnam is led by a multi-party political system. 3. Vietnam’s economic growth is linked to its integration with global supply chains and focus on exports. 4. For a long time, Vietnam’s low labour costs and stable exchange rates have attracted global manufacturers. 5. Vietnam has the most productive e-service sector in the In- do-Pacific region. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1, 3 and 4
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Vietnam has been one of the fastest-growing economies in recent years, with GDP growth averaging 6-7% annually. Strong manufacturing, exports, and foreign direct investment (FDI) have driven growth. Statement 2 is incorrect: Vietnam is a one-party communist state led by the Communist Party of Vietnam (CPV). There is no multi-party system in the country. Statement 3 is correct: Vietnam’s economic growth is highly linked to its role in global supply chains, particularly in electronics, textiles, and manufacturing. It is a key exporter to the US, China, and the EU. Statement 4 is correct: Vietnam has long attracted global manufacturers due to low labour costs, a skilled workforce, and currency stability. Major companies like Samsung, Apple (via suppliers), and Nike have expanded production in Vietnam. Statement 5 is incorrect: While Vietnam has a growing digital economy, it does not have the most productive e-service sector in the Indo-Pacific. Countries like India and Singapore lead in e-services and digital innovation in the region.

subject: International Relations
stem: The term “Levant” often heard in the news roughly corresponds to which of the following regions?
options: Region along the eastern Mediterranean shores
Region along North African shores stretching from Egypt to Morocco
Region along Persian Gulf and Horn of Africa
The entire coastal areas of Mediterranean Sea

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Region along the eastern Mediterranean shores
solution.detail: The Levant refers to the eastern Mediterranean region covering Syria, Lebanon, Jordan, Israel, Palestine and parts of Turkey and Egypt (Sinai Peninsula). It is derived from the French word lever (to rise) and signifies the eastward direction of the rising sun. This region has been historically significant for trade, ancient civilizations, and conflicts. The term is also associated with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL), a designated terrorist organization. Option (b) is incorrect: Region along North African shores stretching from Egypt to Morocco is generally referred to as the Maghreb, not the Levant. The Maghreb includes Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia, Libya, and Mauritania with Egypt sometimes included in broader definitions. Option (c) is incorrect: Region along Persian Gulf and Horn of Africa includes countries such as Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, Yemen (Persian Gulf), and Somalia, Djibouti, Eritrea (Horn of Africa). Option (d) is incorrect: The Mediterranean coastline includes regions from Southern Europe, North Africa, and the Levant but the term “Levant” does not cover the entire Mediterranean.

subject: International Relations
stem: With reference to the “G20 Common Framework”, consider the following statements : 1. It is an initiative endorsed by the G20 together with the Paris Club. 2. It is an initiative to support Low Income Countries with unsus- tainable debt. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: The G20 Common Framework for Debt Treatments beyond the Debt Service Suspension Initiative (DSSI) was introduced in November 2020 to help low-income countries (LICs) manage unsustainable debt burdens caused by economic shocks like the COVID-19 pandemic. Statement 1 is correct: The G20 Common Framework was jointly developed by the G20 and the Paris Club, a group of major creditor nations that coordinate debt relief efforts. It aims to provide debt restructuring, relief, and sustainability measures for eligible countries. Statement 2 is correct: The Common Framework focuses on low-income countries (LICs) with high debt burdens, particularly those eligible for the International Development Association (IDA) assistance. It allows for case-by-case debt restructuring, ensuring that LICs can stabilize their economies and recover from financial crises.

statements.text: The G20 Common Framework was jointly developed by the G20 and the Paris Club, a group of major creditor nations that coordinate debt relief efforts. It aims to provide debt restructuring, relief, and sustainability measures for eligible countries.

subject: International Relations

A permanent American military base has been set up there to help Taiwan to increase its defence capabilities.

solution.detail: The Senkaku Islands (called “Diaoyu Islands” by China) are a group of uninhabited islands in the East China Sea which is controlled by Japan but also claimed by China and Taiwan. The islands are located northeast of Taiwan, east of China, and southwest of Japan’s Okinawa Prefecture. They cover around 7 square kilometers and are strategically important due to their rich fishing grounds and potential oil and gas reserves. subject: International Relations
stem: Which one of the following best describes the term “greenwashing”?
options: Conveying a false impression that a company’s products are eco-friendly and environmentally sound.
Non-inclusion of ecological/ environmental costs in the Annual Financial Statements of a country.
Ignoring the disastrous ecological consequences while undertaking infrastructure development.
Making mandatory provisions for environmental costs in a government project/programme.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Conveying a false impression that a company’s products are eco-friendly and environmentally sound.
solution.detail:
Greenwashing is a deceptive marketing practice where a company misleads consumers into believing that its products, policies, or practices are environmentally friendly, when in reality they are not. It is used to improve a company’s public image without making real sustainability efforts. Companies often use misleading labels, vague terms (like “natural” or “eco-friendly”), or exaggerated claims to appear environmentally responsible. Examples of Greenwashing:
- A company claims its plastic packaging is “biodegradable” but fails to mention that it takes decades to break down.
- Fast fashion brands marketing “sustainable” clothing lines while continuing environmentally harmful practices.
- Oil and gas companies promote carbon offset programs while still expanding fossil fuel extraction.
solution.extraEdge:
- Bluewashing – When companies or organizations exaggerate their social responsibility or commitment to sustainable practices without substantial action, often by associating with the United Nations.
- Greenhushing – When companies deliberately underreport or downplay their sustainability initiatives to avoid scrutiny or accusations of greenwashing.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Gujarat has the largest solar park in India. 2. Kerala has a fully solar powered International Airport. 3. Goa has the largest floating solar photovoltaic project in India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The largest solar park in India is Bhadla Solar Park, located in Rajasthan, not Gujarat. Spread over 14,000 acres, it has a capacity of 2,245 MW and is among the largest in the world. Statement 2 is correct: Cochin International Airport in Kerala is the world’s first fully solar-powered international airport. It was inaugurated as a completely solar-run airport in 2015 and won the UN Champions of the Earth award in 2018 for its sustainable model. Statement 3 is incorrect: Goa does not have the largest floating solar photovoltaic (PV) project. The largest floating solar PV project in India is located in Ramagundam, Telangana, with a 100 MW capacity, developed by NTPC.

statements.text: The largest solar park in India is Bhadla Solar Park, located in Rajasthan, not Gujarat. Spread over 14,000 acres, it has a capacity of 2,245 MW and is among the largest in the world.

Cochin International Airport in Kerala is the world’s first fully solar-powered international airport. It was inaugurated as a completely solar-run airport in 2015 and won the UN Champions of the Earth award in 2018 for its sustainable model.
Goa does not have the largest floating solar photovoltaic (PV) project. The largest floating solar PV project in India is located in Ramagundam, Telangana, with a 100 MW capacity, developed by NTPC.

subject: International Relations
stem: With reference to the “Tea Board” in India, consider the following statements: 1. The Tea Board is a statutory body. 2. It is a regulatory body attached to the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. 3. The Tea Board’s Head Office is situated in Bengaluru. 4. The Board has overseas offices at Dubai and Moscow. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1 and 4
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The Tea Board of India is a statutory body established under the Tea Act, 1953. It is responsible for the promotion, research, regulation, and trade facilitation of the tea industry. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Tea Board functions under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, not the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. Statement 3 is incorrect: The headquarters of the Tea Board of India is in Kolkata, not Bengaluru. Kolkata has been the traditional center of India’s tea trade. Statement 4 is correct: The Tea Board has overseas offices in Dubai and Moscow, which help facilitate tea exports, conduct quality inspections, and enhance international market reach.

subject: International Relations
stem: Which one of the following statements best describes the ‘Polar Code’?
options: It is the international code of safety for ships operating in polar waters.
It is the agreement of the countries around the North Pole regarding the demarcation of their territories in the polar region.
It is a set of norms to be followed by the countries whose scientists undertake research studies in the North Pole and South Pole.
It is a trade and security agreement of the member countries of the Arctic Council.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: It is the international code of safety for ships operating in polar waters.
solution.detail: The Polar Code, implemented in January 2017 by the International Maritime Organization (IMO), is a mandatory regulatory framework for ships operating in Arctic and Antarctic waters. It covers ship design, crew training, pollution prevention, and navigation safety, ensuring environmental protection and human safety in extreme polar conditions. The Polar Code supplements existing MARPOL (Marine Pollution) and SOLAS (Safety of Life at Sea) conventions.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The India Sanitation Coalition (ISC) is an independent platform led by FICCI, aimed at promoting sanitation, but it is not funded directly by the Government of India or WHO. Statement 2 is correct: The National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA) is an autonomous research body under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA). It provides policy recommendations, urban research, and capacity building for cities across India.

subject: International Relations
stem: In India, what is the role of the Coal Controller’s Organization (CCO)? 1. CCO is the major source of Coal Statistics in Government of In- dia. 2. It monitors progress of development of Captive Coal/Lignite blocks. 3. It hears any objection to the Government’s notification relating to acquisition of coal-bearing areas. 4. It ensures that coal mining companies deliver the coal to end users in the prescribed time. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The CCO is the primary source of coal statistics in India, collecting data on production, stock, and dispatch. Statement 2 is correct: It monitors captive coal and lignite blocks, ensuring efficient allocation and compliance. Statement 3 is correct: The CCO hears objections regarding coal-bearing land acquisition, as per the Coal Bearing Areas (Acquisition and Development) Act, 1957. Statement 4 is incorrect: The CCO does not oversee direct coal delivery; this function is handled by Coal India Limited (CIL) and private coal producers. Coal is India’s primary energy source, contributing to over 55% of electricity generation. The Coal Controller’s Organization (CCO) plays a pivotal role in coal regulation, monitoring, and policy implementation.

subject: International Relations
stem: In India, which one of the following compiles infor- mation on industrial disputes, closures, retrenchments and lay-offs in factories employing workers?
options: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Labour Bureau
solution.detail: The Labour Bureau, part of the Ministry of Labour & Employment, is tasked with compiling and analyzing data on industrial disputes, closures, retrenchments, and layoffs in factories with workers. It releases the Industrial Disputes Statistics Report and other labour market indicators, which are essential for informing labour policy decisions in India. These data play a vital role in shaping policies, particularly in monitoring industrial conflicts, workforce reductions, and factory shutdowns. Other options are incorrect: The Central Statistics Office (CSO) focuses on macroeconomic data, not industrial disputes. The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) handles investment and trade policies, not labour issues. National Technical Information System Manpower does not compile labour statistics.

stem: With reference to polyethylene terephthalate, the use of which is so widespread in our daily lives, consider the following statements: 1. Its fibres can be blended with wool and cotton fibres to rein- force their properties. 2. Containers made of it can be used to store any alcoholic bev- erage. 3. Bottles made of it can be recycled into other products. 4. Articles made of it can be easily disposed of by incineration without causing greenhouse gas emissions. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET) is a widely used thermoplastic polymer known for its strength, recyclability, and versatility in textiles and packaging. PET fibres can be blended with wool and cotton to enhance their durability, wrinkle resistance, and strength. It is commonly used in textile manufacturing for blended fabrics. Statement 2 is incorrect: PET containers are not suitable for all alcoholic beverages due to permeability issues and risk of chemical interaction with high-alcohol-content liquids. Statement 3 is correct: PET bottles are 100% recyclable and can be converted into fibres, packaging materials, and other plastic products. Statement 4 is incorrect: Incineration of PET releases carbon dioxide (CO₂) and other greenhouse gases, contributing to environmental pollution.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following: 1. Aarogya Setu 2. CoWIN 3. DigiLocker 4. DIKSHA Which of the above are built on top of open-source digital plat- forms?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2, 3 and 4
solution.detail: Open-source digital platforms enable transparency, scalability, and accessibility in India’s e-governance initiatives. Aarogya Setu (contact tracing for COVID-19) was later open-sourced to enhance security and transparency. CoWIN (COVID-19 vaccination platform) was built using an open-source framework to facilitate large-scale vaccination tracking. DigiLocker (cloud-based document storage) operates on an open-source framework, allowing citizens to access digital documents securely. DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) is an open-source platform for education and teacher training.

Statement 2 is incorrect: ABDM aims to improve accessibility to digital health services, but it is not mandatory for all citizens. subject: Medieval History
stem: Consider the following pairs: King 1. Nannuka Chandela 2. Jayashakti Paramara 3. Nagabhata II Gurjara-Pratihara 4. Bhoja Rashtrakuta Dynasty How many pairs given above are correctly matched?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Only two pairs
solution.detail: Between the 8th and 12th centuries AD, medieval India saw the rise of regional dynasties like the Chandelas, Paramaras, Gurjara-Pratiharas, and Rashtrakutas, which shaped the political, cultural, and architectural landscape after the decline of centralized powers like the Guptas and Harsha’s empire. Pair 1 is correctly matched: The Chandela dynasty was founded around the 9th century, and Nannuka is traditionally regarded as the founder. Chandela dominion extended from the Yamuna (Jumna) River in the north to the region of Saguar (now Sagar) and from the Dhasan River in the west to the Vindhya Hills. It was during Chandela rule that Khajuraho turned into a cultural and architectural hub. The Kandariya Mahadeva Temple stands as the pinnacle of Chandela architectural achievement. The Chandela dynasty is famous for constructing the Khajuraho temples, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Nannuka established the dynasty in the early 9th century CE. Pair 2 is incorrectly matched: Vakpati’s sons Jayashakti (Jeja) and Vijayashakti (Vija) consolidated the Chandella power. Vakapati succeeded his father Nannuka of the Chandela dynasty and he was succeeded by Vijayshakti and Jayshakti. The Paramara dynasty was a prominent Rajput dynasty that ruled Malwa, with kings like Bhoja and Munja being its most famous rulers. Pair 3 is correctly matched: Vatsaraja was a Pratihara ruler who came to the throne in about 778 AD controlled eastern Rajasthan and Malava. Nagabhata II (reigned c. 793–833) was his successor. He was defeated by the Rashtrakuta king Govinda III (reigned 793–814). He ruled in the early 9th century CE and is known for his military campaigns against the Palas and Rashtrakutas. Pair 4 is incorrectly matched: Bhoja I / Mihir Bhoja (c.836−885 AD) was the successor of Nagabhatta II, who had a long reign of over 44-46 years and proved to be the most successful and popular ruler of Pratiharas. An Arab visitor to western India, the merchant Sulaymān, referred to the kingdom of Juzr (which is generally identified as Gurjara) and its strong and able ruler, who may have been Bhoja. The Rashtrakuta dynasty was a prominent power in the Deccan, with kings like Amoghavarsha and Krishna I.

stem: With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements: 1. The first Mongol invasion of India happened during the reign of Jalalud- din Khalji. 2. During the reign of Ala-ud-din Khalji, one Mongol assault marched up to Delhi and besieged the city. 3. Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq temporarily lost portions of the north-west of his kingdom to Mongols. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: The Mongol invasions of India, particularly during the Delhi Sultanate, began in the early 13th century under Genghis Khan and continued through the 14th century. The invasions targeted northern India, with significant assaults during the reigns of Iltutmish, Ala-ud-din Khalji, and Muhammad-bin- Tughlaq. Despite several incursions, the Mongols did not establish permanent control over Indian territories. Statement 1 is incorrect: The first Mongol invasion of India took place in the reign of Sultan Shams-ud-din Iltutmish. Who was a ruler of Slave dynasty and was also able to preserve his kingdom. Statement 2 is correct: During the reign of Ala-ud-din Khalji, Mongols attacked and pillaged not only the surrounding areas, but Delhi itself. Alauddin formed a large standing army for conquest and defense, but the Mongols attacked during his Chittor siege. Statement 3 is incorrect: Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq faced several Mongol invasions during his reign but he successfully defended his kingdom against them and did not lose any territory to the Mongols. In some cases, he even launched counterattacks and drove the Mongols out of the region.

statements.text: The first Mongol invasion of India took place in the reign of Sultan Shams-ud-din Iltutmish. Who was a ruler of Slave dynasty and was also able to preserve his kingdom.

During the reign of Ala-ud-din Khalji, Mongols attacked and pillaged not only the surrounding areas, but Delhi itself. Alauddin formed a large standing army for conquest and defense, but the Mongols attacked during his Chittor siege.

Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq faced several Mongol invasions during his reign but he successfully defended his kingdom against them and did not lose any territory to the Mongols. In some cases, he even launched counterattacks and drove the Mongols out of the region.

subject: Medieval History

stem: With reference to Indian history, who of the following were known as “Kulah-Daran”?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Sayyids
solution.detail:
The 'Kulah-Daran' are a group of Persian immigrants who lived in India in the eleventh century. "Kulah-Daran" literally translates to "hat-wearers" and was used to describe people who wore a specific kind of Persian cap called a "kulah." The Sayyids (1414 to 1451 AD) were known as ‘Kulah Daran’ during the Delhi sultanate. They were the fourth ruling family of the Delhi Sultanate established by Khizr khan. Other dynasties of the Dehi Sultanate are:
- Mamluks (AD 1206–AD 1290)
- The Khaljis (AD 1290–AD 1320)
- The Tughlaqs (AD 1320–AD 1412)
- The Lodis (AD 1451– AD 1526).

subject: Medieval History
stem: In medieval India, the term “Fanam” referred to:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Coins
solution.detail:
Abdur Razzak, was a the Iranian ambassador who visited the Indian subcontinent during the reign of Deva Raya II, noted that in Vijayanagar, three types of coins were prominently used: Varaha, Dinara, and Fanam. Among these, the Fanam was the most widely used.
- The chief gold coin of the Vijayanagar Empire was the varaha or pagoda.
- Tar was a silver coin
- Jittal was a copper coin.

subject: Medieval History
stem: The world’s second tallest statue in sitting pose of Ramanuja was inaugurated by the Prime Minister of India at Hyderabad recently. Which one of the following statements correctly represents the teachings of Ramanuja?
options: The best means of salvation was devotion.
Vedas are eternal, self-existent and wholly authoritative.
Logical arguments were essential means for the highest bliss
Salvation was to be obtained through meditation.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: The best means of salvation was devotion.
solution.detail: Sri Ramanujacharya was born in 1017 AD in Tamil Nadu. He is the most respected Acharya in the philosophy of Sri Vaishnavism. Ramanuja was deeply influenced by the Alvars. Shri Ramanujacharya firmly believed that intense devotion to Vishnu was the best means to attain salvation. He is famous as the chief proponent of Vishishtadvaita subschool of Vedānta. Vishishtadvaita is a non-dualistic school of Vedanta philosophy in which Brahman alone exists, but is characterized by multiplicity. He was also referred to as Ilaya Perumal, which means the radiant one. His works such as Vedartha-Sangraha, the Sribhasya and the Bhagavad Gita-bhashya aimed at providing a philosophical foundation for devotional worship.

stem: With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements: 1. The Dutch established their factories/warehouses on the east coast on lands granted to them by Gajapati rulers. 2. Alfonso de Albuquerque captured Goa from the Bijapur Sultanate. 3. The English East India Company established a factory at Madras on a plot of land leased from a representative of the Vijayanagara empire. Which of the statements given above are correct?
